original_abstract: Multi-query attention (MQA) significantly accelerates decoder inference by utilizing a single key-value head; however, this efficiency often comes at the cost of model quality. Furthermore, the necessity of training a separate model specifically for faster inference is often impractical. In this work, we present two primary contributions: first, a streamlined recipe for "uptraining" existing multi-head language model checkpoints into MQA-capable models using only 5% of the original pre-training compute. Second, we introduce grouped-query attention (GQA), a versatile generalization of MQA that employs an intermediate number of key-value heads. Our results demonstrate that GQA achieves a performance profile nearly identical to multi-head attention while maintaining an inference speed comparable to MQA.
humanized_abstract: While multi-query attention (MQA) gets the decoder inference speedup from just using a single key-value head, it typically comes with a decrease in model performance. In addition, retraining a model to trade off performance for faster inference is generally not an option. This work contains two main contributions. We show a very fast way (requiring only 5% of the original pre-training compute) to “uptrain” a multilo-head language model checkpoint to one that supports MQA. We also introduce a new attention mechanism — grouped-query attention (GQA) — with an intermediate number of key-value heads. While GQA’s performance is almost identical to the original multilo-head attention, it retains the inference speed of the much more efficient MQA.

document.output: While multi-query attention (MQA) gets the decoder inference speedup from just using a single key-value head, it typically comes with a decrease in model performance. In addition, retraining a model to trade off performance for faster inference is generally not an option. This work contains two main contributions. We show a very fast way (requiring only 5% of the original pre-training compute) to “uptrain” a multilo-head language model checkpoint to one that supports MQA. We also introduce a new attention mechanism — grouped-query attention (GQA) — with an intermediate number of key-value heads. While GQA’s performance is almost identical to the original multilo-head attention, it retains the inference speed of the much more efficient MQA.
document.input: Multi-query attention (MQA) significantly accelerates decoder inference by utilizing a single key-value head; however, this efficiency often comes at the cost of model quality. Furthermore, the necessity of training a separate model specifically for faster inference is often impractical. In this work, we present two primary contributions: first, a streamlined recipe for "uptraining" existing multi-head language model checkpoints into MQA-capable models using only 5% of the original pre-training compute. Our results demonstrate that GQA achieves a performance profile nearly identical to multi-head attention while maintaining an inference speed comparable to MQA.
